1.Chase DeLauterLHBCLE vs Keider Montero (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.400 / xBA 0.288 / xSLG 0.549 (+85 pts)Park +9 pts14d 0.330vs RHP 0.353K 11.0%BB 12.9%HR 3.8%Hit 26.5%Edge Hitter

Chase DeLauter LHB vs Keider Montero RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 691+27+0, pitcher rows 626+1549+1571

Verdict

Montero's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Chase DeLauter should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.400 xwOBA (+85 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +47 pts (raw 0.344 -> adj 0.391); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Keider Montero is in a 14-day rough patch (+88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball45.80.3240.351GB 35% / LD 25% / FB 31% / PU 8%0.364+400.4000.2950.5513.8
Changeup20.60.3060.316GB 35% / LD 29% / FB 28% / PU 8%0.403+970.4040.2970.59217.0
Slider16.00.3260.322GB 36% / LD 25% / FB 34% / PU 5%0.361+350.3680.2700.5157.0
Knuckle Curve16.00.3370.337GB 41% / LD 19% / FB 34% / PU 6%0.351+140.3730.2780.5305.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 71.1 mph
swing length 8.1 ft
attack angle 2.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+2.038-2.7on plane
Changeup-6.7+5.817-1.0on plane
Knuckle Curve-9.5+2.6*1-6.9swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-7.8+4.97-2.9on plane
Sinker-5.2-1.114-6.3swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Vladimir Guerrero Jr.RHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.385 / xBA 0.277 / xSLG 0.503 (+70 pts)Park +15 pts14d 0.304vs RHP 0.321K 20.9%BB 6.1%HR 4.3%Hit 29.6%Edge Hitter

Vladimir Guerrero Jr. RHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 745+2875+2643, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Vladimir Guerrero Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.385 xwOBA (+70 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +20 pts (raw 0.347 -> adj 0.367); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Vladimir Guerrero Jr. is on a 14-day slump (-49 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker39.90.3460.323GB 56% / LD 28% / FB 14% / PU 2%0.363+180.3720.3040.4734.8
4-Seam Fastball32.80.3730.285GB 23% / LD 19% / FB 50% / PU 9%0.380+80.3500.2170.51422.1
Sweeper25.70.3360.323GB 34% / LD 37% / FB 27% / PU 3%0.375+390.3840.3120.53844.4
Curveball0.90.3190.307GB 41% / LD 28% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.360+410.3520.2600.49827.1
Changeup0.50.3130.313GB 41% / LD 28% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.363+500.3610.2760.48533.2
Cutter0.20.3520.307GB 41% / LD 28% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.360+80.3530.2730.48526.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 75.0 mph
swing length 7.6 ft
attack angle 1.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-6.2-2.2193-8.5swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.6-3.2205-7.8swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-8.0+8.277+0.2on plane
Changeup-8.1+9.358+1.2on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

3.Christian YelichLHBMIL vs Ben Brown (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.382 / xBA 0.262 / xSLG 0.543 (+67 pts)14d —vs RHP —K 29.1%BB 9.9%HR 8.0%Hit 22.7%Edge Hitter

Christian Yelich LHB vs Ben Brown RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 281+2775+1275, pitcher rows 463+1737+849

Verdict

Projection: 0.382 xwOBA (+67 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.355 -> adj 0.379); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ben Brown is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

4.James WoodLHBWSH vs Nolan McLean (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.371 / xBA 0.253 / xSLG 0.436 (+56 pts)Park +21 pts14d 0.434vs RHP 0.468K 34.1%BB 14.7%HR 4.4%Hit 21.4%Edge Hitter

James Wood LHB vs Nolan McLean RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 961+2923+1406, pitcher rows 833+740+0

Verdict

McLean's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while James Wood should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.371 xwOBA (+56 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.371); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan McLean is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

5.Brett BatyLHBNYM vs Foster Griffin (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.366 / xBA 0.252 / xSLG 0.465 (+51 pts)Park +25 pts14d 0.382vs LHP —K 28.6%BB 6.7%HR 2.1%Hit 27.8%Edge Hitter

Brett Baty LHB vs Foster Griffin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 629+1757+681, pitcher rows 834+0+0

Verdict

Griffin's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Brett Baty should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.366 xwOBA (+51 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +25 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.341); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Brett Baty is on a 14-day heater (+69 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

6.CJ AbramsLHBWSH vs Nolan McLean (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.365 / xBA 0.276 / xSLG 0.419 (+50 pts)Park +20 pts14d 0.270vs RHP 0.276K 24.4%BB 8.6%HR 3.9%Hit 24.0%Edge Hitter

CJ Abrams LHB vs Nolan McLean RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 769+2324+2273, pitcher rows 833+740+0

Verdict

McLean's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while CJ Abrams should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.365 xwOBA (+50 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.337); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.365); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. CJ Abrams is on a 14-day slump (-76 pts vs season). Nolan McLean is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.9 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 10.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.6+6.368+0.6on plane
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+6.3247+2.1on plane
Curveball-9.6+14.364+4.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-6.9+14.440+7.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.3+10.167+4.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.7+14.1102+6.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

8.Daylen LileLHBWSH vs Nolan McLean (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.358 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.420 (+43 pts)Park +17 pts14d 0.366vs RHP 0.395K 23.6%BB 8.8%HR 2.0%Hit 25.8%Slight Hitter

Daylen Lile LHB vs Nolan McLean RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 708+1306+0, pitcher rows 833+740+0

Verdict

McLean's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Daylen Lile should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.358 xwOBA (+43 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.331 -> final 0.358); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan McLean is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

10.Michael Harris IILHBATL vs Braxton Garrett (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.356 / xBA 0.270 / xSLG 0.466 (+41 pts)14d 0.270vs LHP —K 18.8%BB 8.0%HR 2.5%Hit 26.1%Slight Hitter

Michael Harris II LHB vs Braxton Garrett LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 583+2181+1718, pitcher rows 64+0+589

Verdict

Garrett's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Harris II should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.356 xwOBA (+41 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +37 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.350); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.350 -> final 0.356); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Harris II is on a 14-day slump (-114 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

11.Rafael DeversLHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.356 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.467 (+41 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.272vs RHP 0.298K 21.4%BB 9.0%HR 5.8%Hit 23.4%Slight Hitter

Rafael Devers LHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 800+3102+2433, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Rafael Devers should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.356 xwOBA (+41 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.347); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.347 -> final 0.356); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

12.Henry DavisRHBPIT vs Matthew Liberatore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.355 / xBA 0.299 / xSLG 0.500 (+40 pts)Park -15 pts14d —vs LHP —K 18.5%BB 6.3%HR 5.2%Hit 25.6%Slight Hitter

Henry Davis RHB vs Matthew Liberatore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 377+1070+522, pitcher rows 685+2390+1370

Verdict

Liberatore's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Henry Davis should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.355 xwOBA (+40 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +44 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.371); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

13.Kyle ManzardoLHBCLE vs Keider Montero (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.355 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.449 (+40 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.458vs RHP 0.427K 18.1%BB 9.8%HR 4.4%Hit 24.1%Slight Hitter

Kyle Manzardo LHB vs Keider Montero RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 599+2074+709, pitcher rows 626+1549+1571

Verdict

Montero's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Manzardo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.355 xwOBA (+40 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.355); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Manzardo is on a 14-day heater (+133 pts vs season). Keider Montero is in a 14-day rough patch (+88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

14.Ben RiceLHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.354 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.446 (+39 pts)Park +9 pts14d 0.316vs RHP 0.362K 25.9%BB 11.9%HR 4.9%Hit 21.2%Slight Hitter

Ben Rice LHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 739+2129+725, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Ben Rice should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.354 xwOBA (+39 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.345 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.354); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ben Rice is on a 14-day slump (-99 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

16.Daniel SchneemannLHBCLE vs Keider Montero (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.354 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.462 (+39 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.306vs RHP 0.305K 18.7%BB 10.3%HR 3.6%Hit 24.3%Slight Hitter

Daniel Schneemann LHB vs Keider Montero RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 544+1678+934, pitcher rows 626+1549+1571

Verdict

Montero's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Daniel Schneemann should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.354 xwOBA (+39 pts vs league, Hitter). Keider Montero is in a 14-day rough patch (+88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

17.Juan SotoLHBNYM vs Foster Griffin (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.263 / xSLG 0.434 (+38 pts)Park +25 pts14d 0.277vs LHP —K 24.1%BB 12.8%HR 4.3%Hit 21.9%Slight Hitter

Juan Soto LHB vs Foster Griffin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 523+3015+3255, pitcher rows 834+0+0

Verdict

Griffin's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Juan Soto should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: -11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.339 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Juan Soto is on a 14-day slump (-86 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Cutter31.20.3260.330GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.341+150.3560.2790.47031.3
Sweeper22.90.3240.306GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.310-140.3010.2400.38835.7

Split-Finger2.50.3130.313GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.323+100.3210.2560.41227.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

bat speed 70.7 mph
swing length 7.0 ft
attack angle 11.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Cutter-6.5+12.917+6.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

18.Matt ChapmanRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.443 (+38 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.207vs RHP 0.208K 25.3%BB 7.1%HR 4.6%Hit 24.5%Slight Hitter

Matt Chapman RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 775+2213+2722, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Chapman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

19.Jake BurgerRHBTEX vs Sammy Peralta (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.352 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.423 (+37 pts)Park +8 pts14d 0.356vs LHP —K 9.1%BB 4.4%HR 5.5%Hit 27.4%Slight Hitter

Jake Burger RHB vs Sammy Peralta LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 660+1324+2089, pitcher rows 0+165+283

Verdict

Peralta's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Burger should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.352 xwOBA (+37 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.329); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.352); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Burger is on a 14-day heater (+73 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 73.3 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 7.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.5+2.367-2.2on plane
Changeup-7.3+13.446+6.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-6.2+11.122+4.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.6+0.322-5.3swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-6.4+11.511+5.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Misiorowski's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Busch should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.351 xwOBA (+36 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.332 -> adj 0.348); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+129 pts vs season). Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

21.Lenyn SosaRHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.351 / xBA 0.264 / xSLG 0.447 (+36 pts)Park +21 pts14d 0.209vs RHP —K 28.7%BB 2.7%HR 4.4%Hit 29.8%Slight Hitter

Lenyn Sosa RHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 354+2056+1384, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Lenyn Sosa should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.351 xwOBA (+36 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Lenyn Sosa is on a 14-day slump (-41 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker38.50.3360.323GB 56% / LD 28% / FB 14% / PU 2%0.325-100.3330.2920.4235.4

Cutter0.20.3370.307GB 41% / LD 28% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.293-440.2850.2320.37918.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.9 mph
swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 8.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-6.2+4.397-2.0on plane
4-Seam Fastball-4.6+4.7166+0.1on plane
Sweeper-8.0+13.162+5.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-8.1+14.823+6.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Changeup-7.7+11.6*4+3.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

23.Myles StrawRHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.349 / xBA 0.271 / xSLG 0.436 (+34 pts)Park +24 pts14d —vs RHP —K 23.9%BB 4.9%HR 1.9%Hit 27.7%Slight Hitter

Myles Straw RHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 316+1178+14, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.349 xwOBA (+34 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +29 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.332); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.332 -> final 0.349); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker38.80.3020.323GB 56% / LD 28% / FB 14% / PU 2%0.354+530.3620.3250.4491.5

Cutter0.20.3080.307GB 41% / LD 28% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.325+170.3170.2600.41715.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

25.Corbin CarrollLHBAZ vs Landen Roupp (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.346 / xBA 0.265 / xSLG 0.391 (+31 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.446vs RHP 0.478K 20.9%BB 16.9%HR 3.7%Hit 17.3%Slight Hitter

Corbin Carroll LHB vs Landen Roupp RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 720+2514+2649, pitcher rows 859+1853+907

Verdict

Roupp's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Corbin Carroll should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.346); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Corbin Carroll is on a 14-day heater (+65 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

26.Aaron JudgeRHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.346 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.430 (+31 pts)Park +10 pts14d 0.345vs RHP 0.327K 30.8%BB 11.7%HR 6.0%Hit 20.5%Slight Hitter

Aaron Judge RHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 910+2875+3235, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Aaron Judge should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -29 pts (raw 0.353 -> adj 0.323); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.346); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Judge is on a 14-day slump (-84 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

27.Shohei OhtaniLHBLAD vs Griffin Canning (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.345 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.483 (+30 pts)Park -22 pts14d 0.376vs RHP 0.434K 22.0%BB 16.2%HR 5.4%Hit 17.3%Slight Hitter

Shohei Ohtani LHB vs Griffin Canning RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 792+3241+3152, pitcher rows 220+1343+2876

Verdict

Projection: 0.345 xwOBA (+30 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -31 pts (raw 0.380 -> adj 0.349); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.349 -> final 0.345); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

28.Harrison BaderRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.343 / xBA 0.250 / xSLG 0.435 (+28 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.326vs RHP —K 24.1%BB 4.5%HR 3.8%Hit 26.8%Slight Hitter

Harrison Bader RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 288+1961+1693, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Harrison Bader should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.343 xwOBA (+28 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.309 -> adj 0.327); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.343); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

29.Christopher MorelRHBMIA vs Martín Pérez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.343 / xBA 0.261 / xSLG 0.446 (+28 pts)14d 0.160vs LHP —K 23.5%BB 9.6%HR 2.4%Hit 23.5%Slight Hitter

Christopher Morel RHB vs Martín Pérez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 147+1178+2364, pitcher rows 555+904+2208

Verdict

Pérez's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Christopher Morel should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.343 xwOBA (+28 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.340); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Christopher Morel is on a 14-day slump (-48 pts vs season). Martín Pérez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

31.José RamírezRHBCLE vs Keider Montero (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.342 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.408 (+27 pts)Park +15 pts14d 0.409vs RHP 0.388K 12.6%BB 5.2%HR 6.3%Hit 23.5%Slight Hitter

José Ramírez RHB vs Keider Montero RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 899+2816+2971, pitcher rows 626+1549+1571

Verdict

Montero's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while José Ramírez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.325); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Keider Montero is in a 14-day rough patch (+88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Nelson's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Gilbert should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +22 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 33.Ian HappLHBCHC vs Jacob Misiorowski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.341 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.414 (+26 pts)14d 0.349vs RHP 0.386K 40.7%BB 15.3%HR 4.3%Hit 18.1%Slight Hitter

Ian Happ LHB vs Jacob Misiorowski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 863+2839+2728, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Ian Happ should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.341 xwOBA (+26 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 35.Zach ColeLHBHOU vs Zebby Matthews (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.285 / xSLG 0.482 (+25 pts)Park -19 pts14d 0.299vs RHP 0.324K 28.8%BB 5.5%HR 8.6%Hit 21.3%Slight Hitter

Zach Cole LHB vs Zebby Matthews RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 153+224+0, pitcher rows 83+1353+717

Verdict

Matthews' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Cole should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +30 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.359); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

36.Trent GrishamLHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.423 (+25 pts)Park +10 pts14d 0.250vs RHP 0.256K 27.5%BB 16.1%HR 5.1%Hit 16.9%Even

Trent Grisham LHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 718+2529+857, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Trent Grisham should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +21 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.340); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Trent Grisham is on a 14-day slump (-82 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Projected slashxwOBAxBAxSLG
lg 0.315 / 0.245 / 0.4050.3400.2400.423

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 68.5 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 15.0°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.5+14.0187+9.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-7.9+13.445+5.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-10.2+15.0*5+4.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Sweeper-9.1+15.810+6.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.7+12.840+7.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

37.Jazz Chisholm Jr.LHBNYY vs Dylan Cease (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.407 (+25 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.274vs RHP 0.345K 31.2%BB 12.6%HR 6.1%Hit 19.2%Even

Jazz Chisholm Jr. LHB vs Dylan Cease RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 754+2253+2760, pitcher rows 904+3125+3308

Verdict

Cease's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jazz Chisholm Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.340); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

38.Daulton VarshoLHBTOR vs Will Warren (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.340 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.412 (+25 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.347vs RHP 0.356K 23.7%BB 9.3%HR 5.2%Hit 21.6%Even

Daulton Varsho LHB vs Will Warren RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 652+1298+2054, pitcher rows 802+2892+458

Verdict

Warren's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Daulton Varsho should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.340 xwOBA (+25 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.340); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

39.Will BrennanLHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.339 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.448 (+24 pts)Park +4 pts14d —vs RHP —K 15.4%BB 4.7%HR 2.3%Hit 28.0%Even

Will Brennan LHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 36+46+1281, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Will Brennan should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.339 xwOBA (+24 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +21 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.335); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

40.Konnor GriffinRHBPIT vs Matthew Liberatore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.339 / xBA 0.275 / xSLG 0.470 (+24 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.313vs LHP —K 19.6%BB 6.6%HR 0.0%Hit 26.7%Even

Konnor Griffin RHB vs Matthew Liberatore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 625+0+0, pitcher rows 685+2390+1370

Verdict

Liberatore's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Konnor Griffin should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.339 xwOBA (+24 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.334 -> adj 0.346); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

41.Matt OlsonLHBATL vs Braxton Garrett (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.421 (+23 pts)14d 0.330vs LHP —K 21.6%BB 11.2%HR 2.8%Hit 21.9%Even

Matt Olson LHB vs Braxton Garrett LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 913+3172+3000, pitcher rows 64+0+589

Verdict

Garrett's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Olson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.338); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Matt Olson is on a 14-day slump (-53 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

43.Brice TurangLHBMIL vs Ben Brown (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.440 (+23 pts)Park -1 pts14d 0.277vs RHP 0.359K 26.8%BB 11.1%HR 2.0%Hit 24.4%Even

Brice Turang LHB vs Ben Brown RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 802+2922+2528, pitcher rows 463+1737+849

Verdict

Brown's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Brice Turang should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.347 -> adj 0.335); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Brice Turang is on a 14-day slump (-97 pts vs season). Ben Brown is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

45.Casey SchmittRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.277 / xSLG 0.432 (+23 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.313vs RHP 0.349K 23.6%BB 4.4%HR 5.5%Hit 25.0%Even

Casey Schmitt RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 560+1336+374, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Casey Schmitt should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Casey Schmitt is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

46.Austin HedgesRHBCLE vs Keider Montero (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.336 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.394 (+21 pts)Park +20 pts14d —vs RHP —K 21.6%BB 5.5%HR 3.7%Hit 26.2%Even

Austin Hedges RHB vs Keider Montero RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 276+709+598, pitcher rows 626+1549+1571

Verdict

Montero's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Hedges should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.336 xwOBA (+21 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.297 -> adj 0.311); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Keider Montero is in a 14-day rough patch (+88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker35.50.3140.305GB 63% / LD 19% / FB 13% / PU 5%0.302-120.2920.2530.3194.4
Slider29.20.2950.297GB 35% / LD 20% / FB 29% / PU 17%0.303+80.2850.2290.40144.8
4-Seam Fastball23.70.3040.333GB 22% / LD 39% / FB 27% / PU 11%0.352+480.3700.2830.47810.9
Knuckle Curve8.40.2700.311GB 26% / LD 24% / FB 48% / PU 2%0.324+540.3200.2270.44626.1
Changeup3.10.3140.305GB 41% / LD 24% / FB 25% / PU 9%0.328+150.3180.2530.40645.2
Sweeper0.10.2980.304GB 41% / LD 24% / FB 25% / PU 9%0.309+110.2990.2470.39232.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

48.Pete Crow-ArmstrongLHBCHC vs Jacob Misiorowski (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.335 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.419 (+20 pts)14d 0.332vs RHP 0.314K 38.1%BB 6.9%HR 5.6%Hit 22.0%Even

Pete Crow-Armstrong LHB vs Jacob Misiorowski RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 704+2403+1424, pitcher rows 733+1349+0

Verdict

Misiorowski's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +30 pts (raw 0.299 -> adj 0.329); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.335); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jacob Misiorowski is in a 14-day hot stretch (-61 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Verdict

Warren's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Valenzuela should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.322); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Brandon Valenzuela is on a 14-day heater (+83 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 50.Eric HaaseRHBSF vs Ryne Nelson (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.335 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.445 (+20 pts)Park +5 pts14d —vs RHP —K 27.7%BB 4.4%HR 6.9%Hit 22.8%Even

Eric Haase RHB vs Ryne Nelson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 93+339+270, pitcher rows 736+2467+2413

Verdict

Nelson's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Eric Haase should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ryne Nelson is in a 14-day hot stretch (-104 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.
